description_long: This is a freestanding ceremonial banner supported by a wooden pole and base, featuring a horizontal crossbar at the top. The banner itself is rectangular with a pointed bottom edge and displays a detailed emblem, including the text 'IPEC Liberia, Guanacaste' and 'Costa Rica', along with a crest and various symbols. The fabric is bordered in blue and white, with the emblem printed in full color at the center. The support structure consists of a polished wooden pole with decorative finials at both ends of the crossbar and a rounded base for stability. The back of the banner is plain, showing only the fabric without any printed design. This type of banner is typically used for institutional or ceremonial purposes, often seen in schools, government buildings, or public events.
description: A ceremonial freestanding banner with a wooden pole and base, featuring a detailed emblem and text on a blue-bordered fabric. The banner is supported by a polished wooden frame with decorative finials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full emblem, text, and blue-bordered design on the banner fabric.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the wooden pole and base, with the banner fabric barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the banner fabric and the wooden support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden pole and base, similar to view 1, with no visible emblem.
